items: No production hosting is included in this generated output.
No public deployment is recorded by Public Alpha Rehearsal Evidence v0.
The LIVE_ALPHA_01 public-alpha wrapper is a local entrypoint and config guard only; it is not hosting approval.
Public Publication Plane Contracts v0 define route, data, client, and deployment-target governance; the static generator consumes those contracts without changing deployment.
GitHub Pages Deployment Enablement v0 configures static artifact publishing only; it does not host the Python backend or prove deployment success without GitHub Actions evidence.
Static Site Generation Migration v0 generates site/dist for validation, while public_site remains the deployable artifact.
Lite/Text/Files Seed Surfaces v0 adds static compatibility seed surfaces only; it does not add live search, executable downloads, snapshots, relay behavior, or native-client runtime behavior.
Static Resolver Demo Snapshots v0 adds fixture-backed static demo pages only; it does not add live search, a live API, backend hosting, external observations, or production behavior.

Custom Domain / Alternate Host Readiness v0 adds readiness inventory, base-path policy, and operator checklist only; it does not configure DNS, add CNAME, deploy alternate hosts, add provider config, or claim production readiness.
Live Backend Handoff Contract v0 reserves /api/v1 route families only; it does not make /api/v1 live, deploy a backend, enable live probes, or create production API guarantees.
Live Probe Gateway Contract v0 defines source policy, caps, cache/evidence requirements, and operator gates only; it does not implement live probes or make network calls.
Google remains manual-baseline only and is not a live probe source.
Internet Archive live probing remains future, disabled, and gated behind explicit human review.
No live crawling, live source probes, or external source calls are included.
No live source probes are enabled by the wrapper.
No custom domain is configured and no alternate static host is active.
No scraping and no automated Google or Internet Archive baseline collection.
No arbitrary local path access in public-alpha mode.
No auth, accounts, rate limiting, TLS ownership, logging policy, monitoring, or process supervision.
No CORS, auth, or rate-limit policy is finalized for a public live backend.
No production Rust backend.
No native GUI apps.
No installer automation, launch automation, or app-store workflow.
No universal compatibility oracle; compatibility evidence remains source-backed and bounded.
No trust, malware, safety, or reputation scoring.
Fixture-backed evidence is not global truth.
External baselines are manual and time-sensitive until human observation records exist.
